teachingHint: Your child is learning the /ch/ sound. When 'c' and 'h' sit together, they make one new sound: /ch/ (like the start of 'cheese'). It's not a /c/ then /h/ — it's one sound! Help your child spot 'ch' and blend: ch-i-p → chip. 'ch' can be at the start OR end of words: 'rich' ends with /ch/. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: ch-i-p → The 'ch' makes one sound: /ch/. Blend: chip
ch-o-p → The 'ch' makes one sound: /ch/. Blend: chop
ch-i-n → The 'ch' makes one sound: /ch/. Blend: chin
ch-a-t → The 'ch' makes one sound: /ch/. Blend: chat

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'e' says its name 'ee'. We say 'hee'. Learn it by sight.

story: He had a chip on his chin and a chat with a frog. The frog did chop the chip, chop chop, and had a chat on his chin!

ch-i-p → The 'ch' makes one sound: /ch/. Blend: chip

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! We know 'sh' but the 'e' says its name 'ee'. We say 'shee'.

story: A chick had a chip on a chest and she did a check. The chick did check the chest and got a chip!
